hero.title: We Design Space Life
hero.description: Founded in 2014 by Elena Conti, Orenda is a boutique interior studio with fifteen people and two studios — Milan and Geneva. We accept eight commissions a year. Never more.

story.title: Slow Work. Lasting Rooms.
story.quote: "We began in a single room on Via Manzoni. One commission. One drawing table. That commission became Villa Serenoa — and Villa Serenoa became the studio's voice."
paragraphs: We do not rush. We measure. We revise. A room earns its quietness through decisions made slowly — the angle of a window, the grain direction of a floorboard, the temperature of a lampshade at dusk. None of that can be hurried. Each commission begins with weeks of listening before a single line is drawn.

In 2016 we opened in Geneva. Our second studio brought new clients, new materials, and a colder northern light that we found beautiful. Today we work across Italy and Switzerland — residential commissions, private hotels, and the occasional heritage restoration. We keep the team small. Fifteen people is the right size — enough to take on difficult work, small enough that nothing passes without being seen by someone who cares.

founder.name: Elena Conti
founder.title: Founder & Principal
founder.quote: "Eight commissions a year. The limit is not a constraint — it is how we stay good."

stats.label: Interiors Completed
stats.description: Across Switzerland, Italy and the South of France.

stats.label: Client Return Rate
stats.description: Clients who come back. We take that seriously.

stats.label: Commissions Per Year
stats.description: A deliberate limit. Quality demands it.

values.title: Six Principles We Live By.
values.description: We have never written a mission statement. Instead, we keep six principles on the wall of the Milan atelier. They guide every decision — from the first client meeting to the final light check.

items.title: Restraint
items.description: We subtract before we add. The most powerful element in a room is often the one we chose not to include.

items.title: Craft
items.description: Every surface is touched by someone who cares. We work with artisans, not manufacturers. The difference is felt, not seen.

items.title: Patience
items.description: Good interiors take time. We accept eight commissions a year — never more. That limit is not a constraint. It is how we stay good.

items.title: Precision
items.description: A millimeter out of alignment disturbs a room for years. We measure twice. And then once more — because precision is a form of respect.

items.title: Dialogue
items.description: We listen first. The client's life is the brief. Our job is to translate it into space — not to impose a signature, but to reveal a home.

items.title: Longevity
items.description: We design for decades. Not for the photograph — for the morning after, ten years hence, when the room still feels exactly right.

timeline.title: Twelve Years Of Quiet Work.
timeline.description: We do not measure our history by awards or press coverage. We measure it by the rooms we have made and the silence inside them when they are finally finished.

items.title: The Studio Opens
items.description: Elena Conti founded Orenda on Via Manzoni in Milan. Our very first commission is Villa Serenoa at Lake Como: one room, one drawing table.

items.title: Geneva Atelier
items.description: The Geneva studio opens on Rue du Rhône. We take our first hospitality commission Hôtel du Lac, Montreux. A colder light, a welcome addition.

items.title: Casa Bellini & Domus
items.description: Casa Bellini, Rome, is featured in Domus magazine. The studio team grows to eight members now. We are still remaining quite quiet about it.

items.title: Palazzo Nero
items.description: Our largest commission to date, Palazzo Nero, Rome, is completed. A complex heritage conversion that took three years and changed everyone.

items.title: Studio Altura
items.description: Studio Altura wins the Design Excellence Award for Interior Design Italy. Five years without a mediocre commission. We frame it, not hang it.

items.title: Heritage & 41
items.description: Partnership with Swiss Heritage yields three restoration commissions. Our 41st interior is completed. We mark it quietly, the right way.
